name: Pumpkin Cinnamon Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Canned pumpkin or mashed cooked pumpkin
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, heat milk and 2 tablespoons butter just until warm (120°F to 130°F) and butter is almost melted, stirring constantly.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine pumpkin, sugar, and salt. Add the warm milk mixture and beat with an electric mixer until well mixed. Beat in egg and yeast.
3. In a separate mixing bowl, combine flours. Add half of the flour mixture to the pumpkin mixture. Beat on low speed for 5 minutes, scraping sides of the bowl frequently. Add remaining flour and mix thoroughly (dough will be very soft). Turn into lightly greased bowl and grease surface of dough lightly. Cover and let rise in warm place until doubled, about 1 hour.
4. Punch dough down. Turn onto a lightly floured surface. Knead a few turns to form a smooth dough, sprinkling with enough additional flour to make the dough easy to handle. On a lightly floured surface, roll dough into a 12-by-10-inch rectangle.
5. In a small bowl, combine brown sugar and cinnamon. Brush surface of dough with melted butter. Sprinkle with brown sugar mixture.
6. Beginning with the long side of the dough, roll up jelly-roll style. Pinch the seam to seal.
7. With a sharp knife, cut the roll into twelve 1-inch slices. Place rolls, cut side up, in a greased 9-inch square baking pan. Cover and let rise until nearly doubled, 30 to 45 minutes.
8. Bake rolls at 350°F for about 20 minutes or until golden. Remove from pan to a waxed paper-lined wire rack. Cool for 10 to 15 minutes.
Makes 12 rolls.
